notes: Each eval is built as a single self-contained index.html (Tailwind via CDN + Google Fonts allowed, no build step) so every run is uniformly screenshottable. Assertions are written to be checkable from the produced HTML/CSS source and/or the rendered screenshot. Mechanical assertions (radius, gradients, blur, fonts, token usage) are auto-graded by scripts/grade.mjs; visual assertions are for the LLM/human review pass.

evals.prompt: Build an analytics dashboard for a SaaS product called "Northstar". It should have a left sidebar with navigation, a top bar, a few key metrics, a chart area, and a recent-activity list. Make it a single self-contained index.html I can open directly in a browser — you may load Tailwind from the CDN and fonts from Google Fonts, but no build step and no external image hosts. Write the file as index.html in the current directory.
evals.expected_output: A clean, restrained dashboard that reads as a real product surface (think shadcn/ui): neutral palette, hairline borders separating surfaces, modest radii and shadows, no hero block, no gradient/glass decoration, metrics shown soberly rather than as a glossy KPI grid.
assertions: An index.html file is produced and renders without errors
No CSS gradient (linear-gradient/radial-gradient/conic-gradient) is used as a background on the page, sidebar, cards, or metric tiles
No glassmorphism: no backdrop-filter: blur() on panels or the sidebar
Largest border-radius on cards/sidebar/buttons is <= 16px (no rounded-2xl/3xl everywhere)
No box-shadow with blur radius > 24px and no colored (non-black/neutral) shadow
The primary/accent color is neutral or a single restrained hue, not a blue/indigo/cyan brand pop
No hero section or large decorative headline block inside the dashboard
No eyebrow/kicker labels (uppercase + letter-spacing) above headings
Body font is Geist or a system stack, not Inter/Roboto/Segoe UI
Surfaces are differentiated by background token + 1px border, not by floating detached panels

evals.prompt: Build an account settings page with sections for Profile (name, email, bio), Password change, and Notification preferences (a few toggles), plus Save/Cancel actions. Make it a single self-contained index.html I can open directly in a browser — Tailwind from the CDN and Google Fonts are fine, but no build step and no external image hosts. Write the file as index.html in the current directory.
evals.expected_output: A standard, legible settings form: labels above inputs, real focus states, sober section separation, consistent spacing, and unobtrusive Save/Cancel buttons. No floating labels, no gradient buttons, no decorative section notes.
assertions: An index.html file is produced and renders without errors
Each input has a visible label positioned above (or clearly beside) it; no placeholder-only fields and no floating/morphing labels
Inputs have a visible focus state (focus ring or border change)
Buttons use solid/outline fills, not gradient backgrounds, and are not full-width pills
No CSS gradient used as a background anywhere on the page
Largest border-radius on inputs/cards/buttons is <= 16px
The primary/accent color is neutral or a single restrained hue (not blue/indigo/cyan)
Body font is Geist or a system stack, not Inter/Roboto/Segoe UI
No decorative mini-notes or eyebrow labels explaining what each section does

evals.prompt: Build a pricing page for a developer tool with three tiers (Free, Pro, Team), a short header, a monthly/annual toggle, and a feature comparison area. Make it a single self-contained index.html I can open directly in a browser — Tailwind from the CDN and Google Fonts are fine, but no build step and no external image hosts. Write the file as index.html in the current directory.
evals.expected_output: A calm, trustworthy pricing page in the shadcn idiom: a plain headline (no gradient text), three bordered tier cards with one subtly emphasized recommended tier, a simple toggle, and honest feature lists. No gradient hero, no glassmorphism, no glow.
assertions: An index.html file is produced and renders without errors
No gradient text on the headline and no gradient hero background
No glassmorphism (backdrop-filter: blur) on the tier cards
Tier cards are differentiated by border/background and one is emphasized without a glow or colored shadow
Largest border-radius on cards/buttons is <= 16px and cards are not heavily pill-shaped
The primary/accent color is neutral or a single restrained hue (not blue/indigo/cyan)
No random glows, blur haze, or conic-gradient decoration
Body font is Geist or a system stack, not Inter/Roboto/Segoe UI
The recommended-tier emphasis is conveyed with hierarchy (border/weight/label), not a saturated gradient or shadow bloom
